meaning: vt.维护,保护,捍卫n.安全装置,安全措施
examples: The new card will safeguard the company against fraud.
This deal could safeguard the futures of the 2 000 employees.
They will press for international action to safeguard the ozone layer.
He has opened a public consultation on his proposals which he says would safeguard the rights of children.
However, there was a measure of support for closing some properties in order to safeguard others.
